other_observers: none; first found by Henry Armknecht
specific_location: playa 0.4 miles south of CR 10 on CR 23
description: the bird was a medium sized duck, exactly the same size and shape of the adjacent American Wigeon. The bill was blue-gray, and fairly short for a duck.  The head of the bird was rufous-brown, with a contrasting paler buffy crown. The body of the bird was medium gray, with the lower belly and flanks contrastingly white, and the undertail coverts contrastingly black. I saw the bird flap once, and the underwings appeared solid gray, though this bird was far away and it was hard to be certain of this.Behaviors: The bird was swimming near the edge of a playa, mostly obscured by tall grass but at times in the open. It associated with a number of American Wigeon and a few Mallards for the entire observation.Call: none heardPlumage: adult alternate
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer
